calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Advanced From Jan-26-11 To Dec-29-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-30-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for and with Sylvia Sabel (Executor).

The Court stated that the notes had been cleared, that pursuant to California Rule of Court 7.402 that it could not make any finding as to the creditor's claim, and inquired as to why it took so long for the Estate to close which would have bearing on the fees requested. Mr. Dimitriou presented an explanation of the extent of services provided to support the fee request and addressed the length of time it took to close the estate stating that there was a need to probate the decedent's wife's estate, litigate the tax issues, that it took the executor time to go through copious amounts of the decedent's documents which weren't in any order, that they had to determine the tax value of the oil wells owned by the decedent, and that there was other necessary litigation. The Court addressed the claim by the IRS for $74,980.00, the settlement of that claim, and stated that it appeared that it cost the estate $36,000.00 to defend the tax issue only to save $41,000.00. Mr. Dimitriou stated that the cost to defend the IRS issue was actually a little over $10,000.00, that there was other necessary litigation regarding some encumbered assets, that the rates he charged were well below his normal rate, and that there was also litigation to clear the title. At the conclusion of Mr. Dimitriou's explanation/argument, the Court took the matter under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none present.

Ex Parte Application To Advance Hearing Date Of The Petition For Accounting And Atorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Ex Parte Application To Advance Hearing Date
Off Calendar Dec-13-2010 Denied

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Further Report On The Status Of The Administration If The Estate Is Still Not In A Position To Be Closed
Off Calendar Dec-08-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Sylvia Sabel and Norman Sabel (Executors).

Mr. Dimitriou stated that the accounting is ready for filing and that everyone was on board for waiving the accountings. Mr. Dimitriou was directed to file the petition and return an endorsed copy to the Courtroom Clerk so that this matter can be taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (24 Crestlake, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Nov-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Sylvia Sabel. Felix Mendelson interested party.

Mr. Mendelson asked to be allowed to bid on the property. The Court explained that the matter had been previously authorized as a private sale to the Co-Administrator, that the amended petition reflected such and that Mr. Dimitriou had filed the wrong papers. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Continued To May-11-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Sylvia Sabel.

Mr. Dimitriou apologized to the Court stating that there was a calendar error in his office, that he would have the amendment filed by the end of week which will address all of the issues raised in the Examiner's notes, that the sale of the property had not occurred and that the property was still in the Estate. In addition, he stated that the tax matter in the District Court case settled, that the settlement document was circulating, that it was set on the dismissal calendar at the District Court for May 27, 2010, that the sale petition was never ruled on, that it was taken off calendar and that it needed to be filed again. The matter was continued to May 11, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
